sections.paragraph:
μίλιον, ου, τό (Eratosthenes [in Julian of Ashkelon, Metr. Script. I 201]; Polyb. 34, 12, 3; Strabo 3, 1, 9; 5, 1, 11 al.; Plut., Cic. 32, 1, C. Gracch. 7, 3; IGR III, 1385; SIG 888, 26; BCH 29, 1905, 99f; APF 2, 1903, 566 no. 122; PStras 57, 6. Latin loanw.: mille. Loanw. in rabb.) a Roman mile, lit. a thousand paces, then a fixed measure = eight stades = 1,478.5 meters Mt 5:41; D 1:4; GJs 17:2.—M-M.

Julian Julian , various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

IGR IGR = Inscriptiones Graecae ad res Romanas pertinentes—List 3

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

BCH BCH = Bulletin de correspondance hellénique—List 6

APF APF = Archiv für Papyrusforschung—Lists 4, 6

PStras PStras = Griechische Papyrus der kaiserlichen Universitäts- und Landesbibliothek zu Strassburg—List 4

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 651
